event: The Prophet Joel on the Sistine Ceiling
symbols: scroll being read — the prophetic word of God received and proclaimed
red drapery — the blood and fire Joel prophesied would accompany the Day of the Lord
aged face with intense concentration — the weight of prophetic revelation
putti behind — the spiritual realm attending the prophetic office
description: A monumental seated figure of an elderly bald man draped in voluminous red and blue-grey robes, reading intently from an unrolled scroll held in both hands. His brow is deeply furrowed in concentration. Behind him, two smaller youthful figures (putti) peer over his shoulder. The figure is painted within an architectural niche typical of the Sistine Chapel ceiling program. The muscular, powerfully built body and dramatic drapery convey grandeur and authority.
theological_description: The Prophet Joel, one of the twelve Minor Prophets, delivered oracles of profound eschatological significance. His prophecy of the outpouring of God's Spirit — 'I will pour out my Spirit on all people. Your sons and daughters will prophesy, your old men will dream dreams, your young men will see visions' (Joel 2:28) — became the foundational text for understanding the Day of Pentecost, when Peter explicitly cited Joel to explain the descent of the Holy Spirit upon the disciples (Acts 2:16-21). Joel's prophecy spans from immediate agricultural catastrophe (the locust plague) to cosmic eschatology — the great and terrible Day of the Lord when 'the sun will be turned to darkness and the moon to blood' (Joel 2:31). The scroll he reads with such intensity thus contains words that bridge the Old and New Covenants. Michelangelo depicts Joel as a figure of immense intellectual and spiritual power, a man grappling with divine revelation that he can only partially comprehend — words whose full meaning would not be disclosed until centuries after his death, when tongues of fire descended in Jerusalem.

scripture_refs.reason: Joel's prophecy of the outpouring of the Spirit — the central prophetic text associated with this figure
Peter's citation of Joel at Pentecost — the New Testament fulfillment of Joel's prophecy
The locust plague that occasions Joel's prophetic word — the immediate context of his ministry

_meta.title: Sistine Chapel Ceiling: The Prophet Joel
